title: Twist, Lick, Dunk: Oreo Cookie Day!
story: Cookie lovers everywhere celebrate National Oreo Cookie Day on March 6 by doing what they do best - eating America's favorite cookie! People share photos of their Oreo creations on social media, from simple milk dunking to elaborate Oreo cheesecakes and ice cream sundaes. Bakeries and restaurants join the fun by offering special Oreo-flavored treats like milkshakes, cupcakes, and even Oreo pizza! Many families have cookie-eating contests to see who can finish an Oreo the fastest, while others debate the best way to eat them - twist apart first, or bite right in? Some people even stack Oreos into towers or use them for cookie art projects.
activity_question: What do people often debate about when eating Oreos?
activity_choices: Whether to twist them apart first or bite right in
Whether they taste better with orange juice
Whether the cookies should be square instead of round
Whether they're better frozen or heated up
activity_answer: Whether to twist them apart first or bite right in
bonus_fact: Oreos are the world's best-selling cookie brand in over 100 countries!

title: White Chocolate Cheesecake Gets Its Day
story: Creamy white chocolate meets rich cheesecake in the most delicious celebration ever! Every March 6, dessert lovers across America celebrate National White Chocolate Cheesecake Day by indulging in this dreamy treat. Bakeries create special versions with white chocolate drizzles, chunks, or swirled throughout the filling. Home bakers try new recipes, adding berries or caramel for extra flavor. Restaurants often feature it as their dessert special, and social media fills with photos of perfectly sliced pieces. It's the perfect excuse to treat yourself to something sweet and celebrate the magic that happens when two amazing desserts become one incredible creation!
activity_question: What do bakeries often add to their white chocolate cheesecakes on this day?
follow_up_question: What's your favorite way to make cheesecake even more delicious?

title: Frozen Food Gets Its Cool Day
story: Every March 6, people celebrate National Frozen Food Day by appreciating all the icy treats and meals sitting in their freezers! Families might cook frozen pizza for dinner, grab some frozen yogurt, or stock up on frozen vegetables at the grocery store. Some people share photos of their favorite frozen foods on social media, while others try a new frozen meal they've never tasted before. It's a day to remember how frozen foods make cooking easier and keep our favorite treats fresh all year long!
activity_question: What do people often do on National Frozen Food Day?
activity_choices: Share photos of frozen foods on social media
Only eat hot soup all day
activity_answer: Share photos of frozen foods on social media
bonus_fact: The first frozen foods were sold in grocery stores in the 1930s!

title: Ghana's Freedom Day Celebration
story: Every March 6, Ghana celebrates its independence with incredible pride and joy! People across the country wear red, gold, and green clothing to match their beautiful flag. Families gather for huge feasts featuring jollof rice, grilled fish, and sweet plantains. Children wave tiny flags during colorful parades that fill the streets with drumming, dancing, and singing. Many Ghanaians also attend special ceremonies where they remember their freedom and celebrate their rich culture together.
activity_question: What colors do people in Ghana wear to celebrate their independence?
bonus_fact: Ghana was the first African country to gain independence from European colonial rule!
